clinical_history: 35-yr-old: If you could only obtain one lab test what would it be?
diagnosis: Classic (!) ECG for hypercalcemia (13.9 mg/dl here). Note the very abbreviated ST segment such that the T wave looks like it takes off directly from QRS in some leads. High take-off of ST segment in V2/V3 is also sometimes seen in this context. Hypocalcemia is just the opposite: prolonged QT due to long ST segment (corresponding to changes in action potential plateau phase duration).
